phase.name: 1: Fix TagsCell Key Warning in All Tables

task.name: Fix TagsCell read-mode rendering when tags is a string array
task.action:
In `src/components/tables/expenses/ExpenseTable.tsx`, `src/components/tables/income/IncomeTable.tsx`, and `src/components/tables/transfers/TransferTable.tsx`:
1. Import `useTagsQuery` at the top of each file: ```tsx import { useTagsQuery } from '@/hooks/useTagsQuery'; ```
2. Refactor `TagsCell` to be a proper component (not a plain function) so it can use hooks. Convert from `const TagsCell = ({ getValue, row, table }: any) => {` to a proper component.
3. Inside `TagsCell`, call `const { tags: allTags } = useTagsQuery();` to get the full tag list.
4. Normalize the value from `getValue()`: if it's a `string[]` (array of IDs from an in-progress edit), look up the full tag objects from `allTags`. If it's already an `object[]`, use it directly: ```tsx const rawTags = getValue(); const tags: { id: string; name: string; color: string }[] | undefined = Array.isArray(rawTags) && rawTags.length > 0 && typeof rawTags[0] === 'string' ? rawTags.map((id: string) => allTags.find(t => t.id === id)).filter(Boolean) : rawTags; ```
5. In the edit-mode branch, derive `selectedTagIds` from the normalized `tags` array: ```tsx selectedTagIds={tags?.map(t => t.id) || []} ```
6. Apply this identical fix to all three table files.
task.verify: Run `npm run build` — must pass with no TypeScript errors. Manually test: open Expense table, click edit on a row, add a tag, confirm NO "unique key" warning in the browser console.

phase.name: 2: Redesign TagInput — Inline Pills + Focus Fix + Enter Key Fix

task.name: Refactor TagInput to inline pill layout with fixed Enter key and focus logic
task.verify: Run `npm run build` — must pass. Manually test: (1) open a create form, focus tag input with no text — dropdown does NOT flicker; (2) type "Bangkok" and press Enter — pill appears inline; (3) type another tag name, press Enter — second pill appears next to the first; (4) existing tag suggestions appear when typing a partial match.

task.name: Update TagInput.test.tsx for inline pill layout and fixed behavior
task.action:
Update `src/components/shared/TagInput.test.tsx`:
1. Remove the `Input` mock since the component no longer uses the shadcn `Input` — it uses a bare `<input>` element. The placeholder selector `getByPlaceholderText('Add tags...')` should still work on the native input.
2. Update the "renders badge pills" tests — pills are now inside the same container as the input, not in a separate div. The test assertions (checking for badge testids and text content) should still pass since the Badge mock is unchanged.
3. Update the "hides the input when 10 tags are selected" test — the bare input is hidden via conditional rendering, same as before.
4. Add a new test for the Enter key fix: ``` it('selects the existing tag on Enter when input exactly matches a tag name', ...) ``` - Set up tags with "Food", type "Food", press Enter → `onChange` called with `['tag-1']`.
5. Add a new test: ``` it('creates a new tag on Enter when input does not match any existing tag', ...) ``` - Type "NewTag", press Enter → `createTag` called with "NewTag".
6. Update the "does not throw on Enter when input is empty" test — behavior unchanged.
7. Verify the focus-only-when-suggestions test: render with some tags, focus the input → verify the popover opens (the mock always renders, so just verify no error). Render with all tags selected, focus → verify it doesn't trigger open state change.
task.verify: Run `npx vitest run src/components/shared/TagInput.test.tsx` — all tests must pass.

task.name: Run full build, lint, and test suite
task.action: 1. Run `npm run lint` — no new warnings or errors. 2. Run `npm run build` — production build must succeed with zero errors. 3. Run `npx vitest run` — all test files must pass.
task.verify: All three commands exit with code 0.

task.name: Manual smoke test
task.action: With the dev server running (`npm run dev`): 1. **Key warning fix:** Open Expenses table → click edit on a row → add a tag via TagInput in the Tags column → save → confirm NO "unique key" React warning in browser console. 2. **Flicker fix:** Open create expense drawer → focus the Tags input without typing → dropdown does NOT appear and immediately disappear. If existing tags exist, dropdown shows them stably. 3. **Inline pills:** On create expense drawer → type "Bangkok" → press Enter → a pill appears inline in the input container. Type "Food" → press Enter → second pill appears next to the first. Both show colored dot + name + ✕ button. 4. **Enter key logic:** Type the exact name of an existing tag (e.g., "Food") → press Enter → the existing tag is selected (no new tag created). Type a completely new name → press Enter → new tag is created. 5. **Remove pill:** Click ✕ on a pill → pill is removed, tag deselected. 6. **Edit forms:** Open edit expense drawer on a transaction with tags → tags appear as inline pills, can add/remove. 7. **Income + Transfer:** Repeat steps 3-5 on income and transfer create forms. 8. **Table tags:** All three tables show tag badges in the Tags column for tagged transactions.
task.verify: All 8 manual checks pass as described.
